The fixer's fix-log.md, GAPS.json entries, and FINAL-CLOSURE-REPORT.json were read as context/testimony before independent verification and are NOT the basis for any claim below -- every check re-derives its own evidence, including one throwaway reviewer-authored probe test run against both the real fixed module and the real unfixed module (deleted after use, not part of the shipped diff).

checks.description: Confirm MünchenLocation/LocationMünchen/ZürichSalary (both orders, multiple label words/cities) dropped; résumé/München/Zürich/São intact; legit ASCII tech + FP-guard lists (SlideShare/SharePoint/GeoLocation) preserved; C++/.NET/Node.js/CI-CD tokenization unaffected
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Direct calls to `_is_camel_concatenation_artifact()` against the fixed worktree, 2026-07-20T~07:20Z: MünchenLocation/LocationMünchen/ZürichSalary/SalaryZürich/KölnDepartment/GöteborgBenefits/BenefitsGöteborg/MünchenQualifications all => True (dropped). München/Zürich/résumé/São (standalone) all => False (preserved). Full legit-tech list (JavaScript/TypeScript/PostgreSQL/GraphQL/MongoDB/Node.js/GitHub/GitLab/OAuth2/DevOps/iPhone/Golang/Terraform/Kubernetes/C++/C#/.NET/CI-CD) all => False (preserved). FP-guard list (SlideShare/SharePoint/GeoLocation) all => False (preserved). `_WORD_RE.findall()` on 'Experience with C++ and .NET required' / 'Built with Node.js and CI/CD pipelines' / 'C# and F# both count' produces identical continuation-punctuation tokenization to the pre-fix regex (verified by running both the OLD and NEW compiled patterns side-by-side on the same inputs, see check 1c) -- 'C++','Node.js','CI/CD','C#','F#' all stay single tokens, unaffected.

checks.description: Every call site of _WORD_RE checked (_tokens/_meaningful/_jd_keywords, feeding evidence-trace, voice-metrics, and the coverage panel); adversarial probe for new token classes the widened regex surfaces that the old ASCII-only regex fully suppressed (CJK, emoji, other-script digits, Cyrillic/Hebrew/Devanagari) -- does this create a new garbage-chip class?
checks.result: PASS with a documented residual risk (not blocking for this LOW-severity finding)
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] All 3 call sites of `_WORD_RE` (`_tokens()` line 75, `_meaningful()` line 82, `_jd_keywords()` line 327 in the pre-fix file) read and confirmed as the fixer claims -- feeding `_evidence_trace()`, `_keyword_coverage()`, and `_voice_metrics()`. Side-by-side OLD-vs-NEW regex probe on 10 adversarial strings (emoji sequences, emoji+skin-tone modifiers, CJK short/long runs, Cyrillic words, Arabic-Indic digits, Devanagari, Hebrew, NFC-composed accented Latin): emoji sequences and NFC-composed Latin accents tokenize IDENTICALLY before/after (no new tokens) -- confirms the fix does not open an emoji-garbage-chip class. HOWEVER, short non-Latin-script runs previously produced ZERO tokens under the old ASCII-only regex (fully invisible) and NOW tokenize as ONE new token each, some of which pass `_is_semantic_keyword()`: a 4-char CJK company-name-shaped string ('阿里巴巴') => new token, `_is_semantic_keyword` = True; a 5-digit Arabic-Indic numeral run => new token, True; a 4-char Hebrew word => new token, True; a 3-char Devanagari fragment => new token, True. Longer runs (>=6 chars) of these scripts are correctly caught by the PRE-EXISTING `_VOWEL` (ASCII a/e/i/o/u/y only) gibberish guard and dropped (verified: an 8-char CJK run => False). End-to-end test via `_jd_keywords()`/`_keyword_coverage()` on a realistic skill-rich JD (JavaScript/TypeScript/PostgreSQL/DevOps + embedded '阿里巴巴'/'شركة'): the foreign tokens rank 18th/21st in the full ranked list and do NOT reach the top-10 visible panel (real skill terms outrank them via `_skill_score`'s +120 skill-hint / +45 uppercase bonuses, which non-Latin scripts without case cannot earn). BUT on a SPARSE JD (few real keywords, e.g. 'Join 阿里巴巴 team for growth and opportunity in a great culture.'), the CJK token DOES appear in the final ranked/visible list (5 total keywords, all <=10 shown) -- a genuinely NEW, previously-impossible garbage-chip class for short/terse JDs containing embedded non-Latin-script fragments (e.g. a bilingual company name or boilerplate footer). This is real and reproducible, not hypothetical -- but narrow (requires a sparse/terse JD AND embedded non-Latin text), no PII/security implication, and consistent in severity class with the LOW severity already assigned to this finding. Documented as a residual, not treated as blocking, matching this reviewer's own calibration precedent from the NF-final-resid-001 review (exotic real-term false positives noted, not failed, for a LOW/MEDIUM-severity keyword-panel-polish finding).

checks.description: missingResumeNotice() detection must distinguish refusal from genuine success AND from genuine failure
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Traced `resolveRun()` (apps/web/src/lib/api/agents.ts:79-107, UNCHANGED by this branch): on BackgroundJob status='failed' it `throw`s an ApiError BEFORE ever resolving -- so `trigger()`/`pipeline()`'s `output`/`result` can never be a genuine-failure shape; genuine failures are caught in the existing `catch (e) { runErrorNotice(...) }` branch, structurally unreachable by `missingResumeNotice()`. `grep -c missingResume apps/api/app/workers/tasks.py` => 2 hits, BOTH exclusively inside the two `except MissingResumeError` handlers (lines 190, 253) -- confirmed no real-success code path in workers/tasks.py ever sets `missingResume: true`, so `missingResumeNotice()`'s `output.missingResume !== true` check (agents-feedback.ts:161) cannot misclassify a genuine success as a refusal. `missingResumeNotice(output) ?? agentSuccessNotice(...)` / `?? pipelineCompletionNotice(...)` (page.tsx:168,196) is a clean two-branch guard with no overlap possible between the 3 states (refusal / success / failure).

checks.description: runErrorNotice change must not lose the Scout hint where it WAS correct -- check what other 422s reach that path
checks.result: FAIL -- a real, verified, previously-untested regression
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Found the exact case: `resolveParams()` in apps/web/src/app/dashboard/agents/page.tsx (lines 178-187, UNCHANGED by this branch) throws a CLIENT-SIDE synthetic 422 -- `Object.assign(new Error("No jobs discovered yet"), { status: 422 })` -- for `trigger('tailor')`/`trigger('coverLetter')` whenever the user's jobs list is empty. This is a real, reachable, ordinary code path (any user with 0 jobs sourced yet clicking Run on Tailor or Cover Letter), NOT the fitScorer-refusal case this fix targeted. Wrote a throwaway reviewer probe test (`_reviewer-probe-noJobs422.test.ts`, deleted after use, not part of the shipped diff) that constructs this EXACT error object and calls the real, compiled `runErrorNotice()`: against the UNFIXED baseline (99d1a34) it correctly produces `{text:"Tailor needs more data first — run Scout to discover jobs, then try again.", href:"/dashboard/jobs", hrefLabel:"open Jobs →"}` (1 test passed). Against the FIXED branch (8739455), the IDENTICAL input now produces `{text:"Tailor failed — No jobs discovered yet"}` -- NO href, NO hrefLabel (1 test failed asserting href). ROOT CAUSE: `extractApiDetail(err)` (agents-feedback.ts:198-212) returns the raw `err.message` as a fallback whenever the message doesn't end in a JSON `{...}` blob, rather than returning null -- so runErrorNotice's new 422 branch (line ~255) treats this CLIENT-SIDE synthetic Error's plain-text message as if it were a genuine backend-returned detail, takes the `${context} failed — ${detail}` branch, and never reaches the hardcoded Scout-guidance-with-href fallback that was correctly designed for exactly this scenario. NEITHER of the fixer's own new tests catches this: `agents-feedback.test.ts`'s '...falls back to run-Scout-first guidance...' test uses a bare non-Error object `{status:422}` (no message at all, a different code shape than the real client error), and the fitScorer test uses a real backend-shaped 422 with a proper JSON body (the case that SHOULD take the new detail branch). The actual runtime shape thrown by `resolveParams()` -- a real `Error` instance with a plain non-JSON message -- was never tested, and independent testing shows it silently loses its previously-correct, purpose-built href/hrefLabel navigation CTA and its directive 'run Scout...then try again' copy, replaced by a less-actionable, un-clickable message. This is not a reintroduction of dishonesty (the new text is still truthful) but it IS a concrete, reproducible, untested UX/functional regression for a real and ordinary (not exotic) code path, which the coordinator's brief explicitly asked to be checked for.

checks.description: Verify the 3 email modes' exception paths yourself in the backend (EmailAgentError not MissingResumeError for draft_reply)
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Read apps/api/app/agents/email_agent.py in full for all 3 modes: `_triage()` (line 191) and `_insights()` (line 351) never call `_resume_text()`/resume grounding at all -- confirmed by reading both full method bodies, no résumé dependency exists structurally. `_compose_draft()` (line 280, used by `draft_reply`) calls `self._resume_text(user_id)` (line 294) -> `resolve_user_resume_text(user_id, allow_operator_fallback=False)` (apps/api/app/services/resume_grounding.py:43-63) which returns `""` on empty (confirmed by reading the function body: `if not allow_operator_fallback: return ""`, never raises). `_compose_draft` then does its OWN check (line 295-296): `if not resume_text.strip(): raise EmailAgentError("Add your resume before drafting a reply.")`. `EmailAgentError` (email_agent.py:86) is confirmed `class EmailAgentError(ValueError)` -- NOT `MissingResumeError`, no shared base class. `grep -n 'MissingResumeError' apps/api/app/workers/tasks.py` shows it is imported specifically from `app.services.resume_grounding` (line 155) and only 2 `except MissingResumeError` clauses exist (lines 175, 240); an `EmailAgentError` instance does not match either and falls through to the generic `except Exception as exc:` (line 262), which calls `repo.mark_failed(job_id, ...)` -- job status becomes 'failed', NOT 'completed' with `missingResume:true`. `resolveRun()` throws an ApiError on `status==='failed'`, which email/page.tsx's existing `generateDraft()` catch (`setDraftError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : ...)`, line 212-213) already surfaces honestly. `grep -n 'agents-feedback\|agentSuccessNotice\|pipelineCompletionNotice\|runErrorNotice\|missingResumeNotice' apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx` => zero matches -- email/page.tsx does not import or use the shared notice mechanism this fix touches AT ALL, confirming there is no shared surface to have left broken. Email-page immunity claim independently and fully confirmed.

checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] `git diff 99d1a34..fix/nf-final-closure | grep -nE 'TODO|FIXME|@ts-ignore|eslint-disable|as any\b|Math\.random|--no-verify|console\.log\('` => only one hit, `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` in the new `agents/__tests__/page.test.tsx`, immediately before the mocked-module import -- the same established single-rule, test-only, vi.mock-then-import convention already independently verified present in 18+ other test files during this reviewer's prior NF-final-resid review; not a new or novel suppression. No `any` casts (only false-positive hits on the English word 'any' inside doc comments). No Math.random, no fake scores, no --no-verify, no TODO/FIXME stubs, no suppressed-error patterns.

verdict_reasons: FAIL. NF-final-closure-001 (backend) independently verified PASS in full: fail-before (10/35) and pass-after (45/45) reproduced exactly; the reported MünchenLocation/LocationMünchen repro and 7 adversarial variants (both gluing orders, 4 accented cities, multiple label words) all correctly dropped; résumé/München/Zürich/São preserved; the full legit-tech and FP-guard allowlists preserved; C++/.NET/Node.js/CI-CD tokenization unaffected; 162/162 broader regression clean. One residual, narrow, LOW-materiality risk documented (short non-Latin-script tokens can now surface as chips in sparse JDs, previously fully invisible) -- consistent with the finding's own LOW severity and this reviewer's established calibration for exotic/narrow residuals, not blocking on its own.

NF-final-closure-002 (frontend) has a real, independently-and-conclusively-verified regression: the exact client-side 'No jobs discovered yet' synthetic 422 that `resolveParams()` throws for Tailor/CoverLetter when a user has zero jobs sourced -- the GENUINE, ordinary, pre-existing 'run Scout' scenario the hardcoded copy was originally built for -- silently loses its actionable `/dashboard/jobs` href/hrefLabel and its directive 'run Scout to discover jobs, then try again' copy after this fix, replaced by a bare, unclickable 'Tailor failed — No jobs discovered yet'. Reproduced with a direct before/after test against the real compiled module (not simulated): PASS at 99d1a34 (href present), FAIL at 8739455 (href absent) for the identical input. This exact runtime shape (a real Error with a plain non-JSON message, thrown client-side) is not covered by either of the fixer's own new tests (one uses a bare non-Error `{status:422}` object with no message, the other uses a full JSON-bodied backend 422) -- a genuine gap in the fixer's own adversarial self-testing that this independent review exists to catch. This is not a reintroduced dishonesty/false-success (the new message is still truthful) but it IS a concrete, reproducible, easily-reachable (any new user with 0 jobs) functional/UX regression that the coordinator's brief explicitly asked to be checked for, and it was found.

missingResumeNotice()'s refusal/success/failure discrimination is sound and independently verified (structurally cannot misclassify any of the 3 states). The email-page immunity claim is independently and fully confirmed at the code level (EmailAgentError vs MissingResumeError, job status 'failed' not 'completed', and email/page.tsx's total non-use of the shared notice mechanism).
Scope is exactly the 6 claimed files; no prohibited patterns beyond an already-established, non-novel test-only eslint-disable convention.
required_changes_for_pass: In apps/web/src/lib/agents-feedback.ts, fix runErrorNotice()'s 422 branch (around the `const detail = extractApiDetail(err); if (detail) {...}` block added by this fix) so it only takes the 'surface the real detail' path when the detail was genuinely extracted from a parsed JSON `{"detail": ...}` body (a real backend-returned 422), NOT when it is merely a raw, non-JSON Error.message (which includes the pre-existing client-side synthetic 'No jobs discovered yet' error thrown by resolveParams()). Concretely: either (a) add a stricter helper (e.g. extractApiJsonDetail) that returns null instead of falling back to the raw message when no `{...}` JSON is found, and use that helper (not extractApiDetail) in this one call site, or (b) special-case the resolveParams 'No jobs discovered yet' error (e.g. give it a distinguishing marker/property, or check err.message against a known synthetic-error string) before falling through to extractApiDetail, so it keeps producing the original href-bearing Scout-guidance notice. Do NOT change extractApiDetail's general behavior or providerCredentialErrorNotice's usage of it (that call site's fallback-to-raw-message behavior is unrelated and intentionally different).

Add a regression test for this exact scenario: runErrorNotice(Object.assign(new Error('No jobs discovered yet'), {status:422}), 'Tailor') must still produce a notice with href:'/dashboard/jobs' and the original directive copy -- distinct from both of the existing 422 tests (the bare-object no-message case, and the real-backend-JSON-detail case).

No other change required for NF-final-closure-002. NF-final-closure-001 requires no change to reach PASS (its residual finding is documented, not blocking); an optional follow-up (not required for this review's PASS) would be to also gate the label-word branch behind a script-aware check or extend the len>=6 gibberish guard to catch short non-Latin runs, if the orchestrator judges the residual worth closing now rather than deferring.

re_review.commit_subject: fix(NF-final-closure-002): preserve Scout-guidance notice for client-side zero-jobs 422 (review regression)

verdict_reasons: PASS. All 4 requested checks confirmed independently: the diff 8739455..87590d9 touches only agents-feedback.ts and its test, with extractApiDetail/providerCredentialErrorNotice provably untouched; this reviewer's own original regression-reproducing probe now yields the correct href-bearing Scout notice when re-run directly against the real compiled module at 87590d9; the genuine backend JSON-detail case (fitScorer) still surfaces the honest detail with no href, confirming the fix's original intent is preserved; fail-before (1 failed/20 passed) and pass-after (21/21) independently reproduced exactly; full vitest (477/477) and tsc --noEmit (clean) independently reproduced exactly.

This closes the single required_changes_for_pass item from the initial FAIL verdict. Combined with the already-PASS NF-final-closure-001 backend finding, branch fix/nf-final-closure (019a9e9 + 8739455 + 87590d9) is deploy-ready.
